tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal near-term directional conviction, with the median at $69,145 essentially flat to spot across 90 markets with $12.1M in volume. The interquartile range of $68,278-$70,030 reflects tight uncertainty, with 80% of outcomes bounded between $67,070 and $71,329.

Markets maintain flat positioning at $69,130 median, suggesting one-day price forecasts are anchored to current levels with similar distribution width as April 1st. The 10th-90th range expands modestly to $66,178-$71,965, indicating marginally increased tail risk.

A modest upside bias emerges with the median rising to $69,214 (+0.1%), though conviction remains limited as the interquartile range ($67,220-$71,217) shows comparable dispersion. The expanded 90th percentile to $73,122 suggests some market participants pricing in larger rallies within this short window.

Markets show near-flat expectations at $69,224 median with a notably compressed upper bound, as the 75th and 90th percentiles both anchor at $70,000. This suggests diminishing probability of significant upside moves and potential technical resistance at the $70K level.

Year-end 2026 forecasts show sharply bearish positioning with a median of $47,143 (-31.8% from spot) and significant fragmentation—the 25th percentile at $40,000 implies roughly 25% tail risk to sub-$40K levels, while the 90th percentile extends to $109,999, indicating bifurcated market views on longer-term outcomes. Note: zero markets reported for this tenor despite inclusion, warranting caution on the reliability of this projection.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect WTI to rally to a median of $114 (+17.8%) by end-April 2026, with relatively tight consensus across 5 markets ($774.9M volume). The 25th-75th percentile range of $78-$128 suggests moderate conviction around a near-term recovery, though tail risks extend to $147 on the upside and $69 on the downside.

By end-June 2026, prediction markets show a dramatic shift lower to a median of $76 (-22.0% from spot) across 14 markets ($551.9M volume), indicating expected normalization after the April peak. The wide 25th-75th range of $63-$141 and extreme tail scenarios ($56-$196) reveal substantial disagreement about mean reversion dynamics two quarters out.

tenors.narrative: Markets are pricing a modest 3.1% decline in gold over the next 18 months, with a median expectation of $4,566/oz. The wide interquartile range ($3,929-$5,669) reflects significant uncertainty, with the 10th-90th percentile spanning $3,800-$5,970, suggesting institutional participants see meaningful tail risks in both directions despite the bearish central scenario.
